MINI-MINUTES FOR OCT-05-2007 9:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT BLUE SHIELD OF CALIFORNIA LIFE & HEALTH, CALIFORNIA PHYSICIANS SERVICE DBA BLUE SHIELD OF CALIFORNIA'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. COURT MODIFIES ITS TENTATIVE RULING: DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND IN 10 DAYS AS TO CAUSE OF ACTIONS 1, 5, 8, 10 AND 11. SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO CAUSE OF ACTIONS 2, 3, 6 AND 9. THERE IS NO CAUSE OF ACTION FOR CONVERSION BY THEFT. AN ASSIGNMENT DOES NOT CREATE A FIDUCIARY DUTY AS TO 1ST AND 5TH CAUSE OF ACTIONS, PLAINTIFF MUST ALLEGE FACTS THAT DEFENDANT HAD ACTUAL KNOWLEDGE. THE COMPLAINT DOES EXPLAIN HOW DEFENDANT IS UNJUSTLY ENRICHED GIVEN THAT IT OWES THE FUNDS TO THE CLAIMANT OR MUST ESCHEAT THE FUNDS TO THE STATE AFTER 7 YEARS. IF PLAINTIFF IS ABLE TO STATE A CLAIM FOR CONVERSION, PLAINTIFF MAY BE ABLE TO STATE A CLAIM FOR UNFAIR BUSINESS PRACTICES. THE COURT FAILED TO ADDRESS CAUSE OF ACTION # 7 IN THE TENTATIVE. IT IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND FOR THE SAME REASONS AS CAUSE OF ACTIONS 2, 3, 6 AND 9. (PREVAILING PARTY IS TO SUBMIT A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR #8600

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT CALIFORNIA PHYSICIANS' SERVICE'S (D.B.A. BLUE SHIELD, BLUE SHIELD OF CALIFORNIA LIFE & HEALTH) DEMURRER TO SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT. NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING: SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFF IS UNABLE TO PLEAD THE REQUISITE KNOWLEDGE. CASEY V U.S. BANK NAT'L. ASSOCIATION (2OO5) 127 CAL APP 4TH 1138, 1144. PLAINTIFF'S UNFAIR COMPETITION IS BARRED BY APPLICABLE CASE LAW. LORI RUBINSTEIN PHYSICAL THERAPY V P.T.P.N., INC. (2007) 148 CAL APP 4TH 1130, 1142; CEL-TECH COMMUNICATIONS, INC. V LOS ANGELES CELLULAR TELEPHONE CO. (1999) 20 CAL 4TH 163, 182. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR #8600

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Feb-08-2010 Off Calendar

Default Prove-Up Hearing As To Defendant Reynaldo Castillo, Only.
Off Calendar Feb-04-2010 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled this day for Default Prove-Up Hearing as to defendant Reynaldo Castillo, only.
Dept. 218, Comm. William R. Gargano, Clerk: Kevin Dougherty, CSR: Lois Jones, #3689.

Plaintiff Richard B. Fox, M.D. was present in pro per, placed under oath and examined.
No appearance by defendant Reynaldo Castillo.
Default was entered as to defendant Reynaldo Castillo on March 4, 2008.
Dismissal with prejudice as to defendant Shelia Sabatino was filed on May 14, 2007.
Dismissal without prejudice as to defendant Devon Good was filed on December 12, 2007.
Defendants' Blue Shield of California Life & Health, and California Physicians Service, dba Blue Shield of California, demurrer to plaintiff's second amended complaint was sustained without leave to amend on Apirl 7, 2008.
Order of Dismissal as to defendants Blue Shield of Californa Life & Health Insurance Company, and California Physicians' Service, dba Blue Shield of California, was filed on July 25, 2008.

After hearing the evidence presented, and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of Plaintiff Richard B. Fox, M.D., and against the Defaulted Defendant Reynaldo Castillo as follows:

Damages/Principal - $3,490.00.
Costs - $275.00, for a total judgment in the amount of $3,765.00.

Judgment was submitted to and signed by the Court.

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-07-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. COURT MODIFIES ITS TENTATIVE RULING:

DEFENDANT BLUE SHIELD OF CALIFORNIA LIFE & HEALTH, CALIFORNIA PHYSICIANS SERVICE DBA BLUE SHIELD OF CALIFORNIA'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND IN 10 DAYS AS TO CAUSE OF ACTIONS 1, 5, 8, 10 AND 11. SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO CAUSE OF ACTIONS 2, 3, 6 AND 9. THERE IS NO CAUSE OF ACTION FOR CONVERSION BY THEFT. AN ASSIGNMENT DOES NOT CREATE A FIDUCIARY DUTY AS TO 1ST AND 5TH CAUSE OF ACTIONS, PLAINTIFF MUST ALLEGE FACTS THAT DEFENDANT HAD ACTUAL KNOWLEDGE. THE COMPLAINT DOES EXPLAIN HOW DEFENDANT IS UNJUSTLY ENRICHED GIVEN THAT IT OWES THE FUNDS TO THE CLAIMANT OR MUST ESCHEAT THE FUNDS TO THE STATE AFTER 7 YEARS. IF PLAINTIFF IS ABLE TO STATE A CLAIM FOR CONVERSION, PLAINTIFF MAY BE ABLE TO STATE A CLAIM FOR UNFAIR BUSINESS PRACTICES. THE COURT FAILED TO ADDRESS CAUSE OF ACTION # 7 IN THE TENTATIVE. IT IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND FOR THE SAME REASONS AS CAUSE OF ACTIONS 2, 3, 6 AND 9. (PREVAILING PARTY IS TO SUBMIT A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600 (302/PJM/AY)
